title: S2 E6 June Book Club: Love Overboard
description: This week we are chatting about our June Book Club pick, Love Overboard by Kandi Steiner. Spoiler alert: we have a lot of feelings about this one! This book is perfect for fans of Below Deck. As always this episode contains major book spoilers. Podcast Instagram: ⁠⁠⁠⁠https://www.instagram.com/trigger.warnings.bookclub/ ⁠⁠⁠⁠ Andrew's instagram if you want to contact him for all your editing or content needs: ⁠⁠⁠⁠https://www.instagram.com/andrews_edit?igsh=MXdrZ3B0Y3k2a2R4dw==⁠⁠⁠⁠ Want to be a guest on the podcast? Fill out our interest form: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLScCT-Xqb8kexY97qp2JL5TRoUYBadHl3IPjqqRVSpb_dnHIgA/viewform?usp=header If you are interested in donating to Holly's marathon fundraiser: ⁠Holly Riccio's fundraising page for Book Fairies⁠ Book Fairies sources and distributes books in under resourced communities across NYC, Long Island, and internationally and I am so excited to be running and fundraising for this organization! Music credit: "Jardins du Luxembourg" by Jahzzar ⁠⁠⁠⁠https://freemusicarchive.org/music/Jahzzar/contact⁠

title: S2 E5 Book Review: The MindFck Series
description: This week we are discussing the MindFck series by S.T Abby. Spoiler alert: CJ and I are huge fans although we caution interested readers to search out trigger warnings first because this series is seriously dark and not for the faint of heart. As always this episode contains major spoilers so listen with caution. Podcast Instagram: ⁠⁠⁠https://www.instagram.com/trigger.warnings.bookclub/ ⁠⁠⁠ Andrew's instagram if you want to contact him for all your editing or content needs: ⁠⁠⁠https://www.instagram.com/andrews_edit?igsh=MXdrZ3B0Y3k2a2R4dw==⁠⁠⁠ If you are interested in donating to Holly's marathon fundraiser: Holly Riccio's fundraising page for Book Fairies Music credit: "Jardins du Luxembourg" by Jahzzar ⁠⁠⁠https://freemusicarchive.org/music/Jahzzar/contact⁠

title: S2 E4: He's a 10 but...
description: This week we are hazing Andrew by making him participate in a game of he's a 10 but... A few of the books we mention include The Pucking Wrong Number, Zodiac Academy, and Pestilence. Don't feel too bad for Andrew- he hazed CJ right back by keeping in an episode blooper at the start. Podcast Instagram: ⁠⁠https://www.instagram.com/trigger.warnings.bookclub/ ⁠⁠ Andrew's instagram if you want to contact him for all your editing or content needs: ⁠⁠https://www.instagram.com/andrews_edit?igsh=MXdrZ3B0Y3k2a2R4dw==⁠⁠ Music credit: "Jardins du Luxembourg" by Jahzzar ⁠⁠https://freemusicarchive.org/music/Jahzzar/contact

title: S2 E3: Interview with Silent Book Club of Monmouth County
description: This week we are joined by two exciting guests: Nicole and Amy, two of the co-leaders of Silent Book Club of Monmouth County. They told us all about what silent book club is, some exciting meetups they have planned for the summer, and chatted about their favorite authors and books. Silent Book Club of Monmouth County Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/silentbookclub_moco_nj/?hl=en Silent Book Club of Monmouth County Website: https://silentbookclubmoncty.carrd.co/ Podcast Instagram: ⁠⁠https://www.instagram.com/trigger.warnings.bookclub/ ⁠⁠ Andrew's instagram if you want to contact him for all your editing or content needs: ⁠⁠https://www.instagram.com/andrews_edit?igsh=MXdrZ3B0Y3k2a2R4dw==⁠⁠ Music credit: "Jardins du Luxembourg" by Jahzzar ⁠⁠https://freemusicarchive.org/music/Jahzzar/contact⁠

title: S2 E2 May Book Club: The Seven Year Slip
description: This week we are discussing our May book club pick, The Seven Year Slip, by Ashely Poston. This is a magical realism romance set in NYC dealing with some heavy topics such as grief. As always this episode contains major spoilers. Andrew also shares his thoughts on Off Campus Episode 1. Podcast Instagram: ⁠https://www.instagram.com/trigger.warnings.bookclub/ ⁠ Andrew's instagram if you want to contact him for all your editing or content needs: ⁠https://www.instagram.com/andrews_edit?igsh=MXdrZ3B0Y3k2a2R4dw==⁠ Music credit: "Jardins du Luxembourg" by Jahzzar ⁠https://freemusicarchive.org/music/Jahzzar/contact

title: S2 E1 Off Campus
description: Welcome to Season 2 of the podcast! We are kicking things off with a deep dive on the new Off Campus show. As always this episode contains spoilers (for both the show and the entire book series) We also welcomed the newest member of our team: our new editor, head of all things technology, and non-reader, Andrew. Podcast Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/trigger.warnings.bookclub/ Andrew's instagram if you want to contact him for all your editing or content needs: https://www.instagram.com/andrews_edit?igsh=MXdrZ3B0Y3k2a2R4dw== Music credit: "Jardins du Luxembourg" by Jahzzar https://freemusicarchive.org/music/Jahzzar/contact

title: Book Review: The Pucking Wrong Number
description: This week we are discussing a book that made Caitlyn so angry that I knew I had to read it: The Pucking Wrong Number. It's part hockey romance, part dark romance and 100% over the top. Go in knowing you're going to have a laugh and you might enjoy it like I did. As always this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Author Interview: Cassie Swindon
description: This week we are joined by the fabulous Cassie Swindon, an indie author in North Carolina! Cassie writes fantasy romance and also organizes Oak and Ink, pop-up book markets in the Triangle area of NC. Cassie was kind enough to tell us a bit about herself, her process as an author, her books, and her hilarious cats! Check out one of Cassie's books and if you're local to NC we highly recommend attending an Oak & Ink event (she gives a run through of the upcoming calendar in this episode) A few of Cassie's links: Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/cassie_swindon_author Oak and Ink Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/oakandink_bookish Cassie's website: www.cassieswindon.com

title: April Book Club: Things We Left Behind
description: This week we finish discussing the Knockemout series by Lucy Score. This was our favorite book of the entire series and we are so sad to see it ending. As always this episode contains major book spoilers so listen with caution.

title: Book Review: The Last Letter
description: This week we are discussing the emotionally traumatizing book, The Last Letter, by Rebecca Yarros. This book hurt our feelings over and over again and yet? We liked it. As always this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Book Review: In Her Own League
description: This week we are talking about In Her Own League by Liz Tomforde, one of our favorite authors. We thoroughly enjoyed this spicy sports romance but we are so sad to officially close the chapter on the Windy City series! As always this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Reading One Star Reviews (Again)
description: What can we say? We love hurting our own feelings. This episode contains some spoilers (although not as bad as usual!)

title: March Book Club: Quicksilver
description: This week we are discussing our March book club pick- Quicksilver! This book got both of us out of a reading slump and overall we really enjoyed it although it wasn't quite a 5 star read for either of us. As always this episode contains spoilers.

title: Book Review: Assassin's Blade
description: This week we are discussing Assassin's Blade (and a little bit of Crown of Midnight) from the Throne of Glass Series! As always this episode contains spoilers (and Holly trying really hard to not spoil any of the rest of the series for CJ) so listen at your own risk if you haven't read all of TOG

title: Finding your Book Community
description: Today we are joined by some friends and family of the Pod (including a returning guest you might remember- Adrian Andover!) We are discussing how we have found our various book communities and give some fun ideas for book club activities!

title: Spring TBR 2026
description: This week we are planning our Spring 2026 TBRs. We've included books by some of our favorite authors (looking at you Liz Tomforde) as well as some discoveries!

title: Book Review: Pestilence
description: This week we are discussing Pestilence by Laura Thalassa. We never thought we'd be swooning and laughing over a horseman of the apocalypse and yet here we are. Hold on tight this episode is a weird one (and of course contains spoilers).

title: February Book Club: Blood of Hercules
description: This week we are discussing our February book club pick- Blood of Hercules. We had mixed feelings about this one and yet immediately downloaded book 2 in the series so clearly it did something right. As always this episode contains major spoilers

title: Best of: Romantasy
description: This week we are sharing some of our top fantasy romance recommendations for all your moods! Categories include: if you're looking for something messy, enemies to lovers, and stories where romance is more of the secondary plot.

title: Love is in the Air
description: Happy Valentine's Day! Love is in the air this week as we discuss some of our top romance recommendations. Whether you're in the mood for sports, cozy fantasy, literary fiction with some romance, or more- we've got you.

title: Book Review: Spark of the Everflame
description: This week we are discussing Spark of the Everflame by Penn Cole. This is the start of an ongoing slow-burn, enemies to lovers fantasy series and our in person book club had a lot of feelings on it. As always this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Book Review: Caught Up
description: This week we are reviewing Caught Up by Navessa Allen! This is the second book following Lights Out and takes a slightly darker turn as our MMC is in the mob. While we didn't love it as much as Lights Out we'd still highly recommend! As always this epsidoe contains major spoilers.

title: January Book Club: Maneater
description: This week we are discussing our January book club pick, Maneater by Morgan Elizabeth. While this might not have been our favorite Morgan Elizabeth book yet we really enjoyed it and think it is a fun romantic suspense book! As always this episode contains major spoilers so listen with caution.

title: 2026 Anticipated New Releases
description: This week we are talking about some of our most anticipated new releases for 2026 (for some reason a lot of them come out in March?). Featured authors include Navessa Allen, Morgan Elizabeth, and of course Liz Tomforde to name a few.

title: Book Review: Problematic Summer Romance
description: This week we are discussing Problematic Summer Romance by Ali Hazelwood. Spoiler alert we have conflicting feelings about this one. While it is not our favorite Ali Hazelwood book (shoutout Deep End) we overall still enjoyed many parts of it. As always this episode contains spoilers.

title: 2025 Wrap-Up
description: This week we are discussing how 2025 went for us reading wise. We reflect on the year and share some of our top reads. We close out by discussing our goals and predictions for 2026!

title: 26 in 2026
description: This week we are discussing what is on our 26 books to read in 2026 list! We also go over the results of our 25 in 2025 lists.

title: December Book Club: The Gargoyle Grinch
description: Happy Holidays! This week we are discussing our December book club pick- The Gargoyle Grinch by Lilith Stone. This is pure holiday monster romance and we enjoyed it (although it left us with some questions about gargoyles). As always this episode contains spoilers.

title: Book Review: Big Nick Energy
description: This week we are celebrating the holidays with a Morgan Elizabeth Christmas special! We discuss one of our favorite holiday romances- Big Nick Energy. It's got some of our favorite tropes- single parents, he's immediately down bad, and ex's dad (kind of). As always this episode contains spoilers.

title: Just Smut No Plot
description: This week we are giving some recommendations for books that are heavy on the smut and light on the plot for when you're looking for some easy reading. They're perfect for anyone trying to reach their reading goal last minute.

title: November Book Club: Wilder
description: This week we are discussing our November book club pick, Wilder by Rebecca Yarros. This emotional romance really left a mark on us! As always this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Holiday TBR
description: This week we are sharing what books are on our TBR for the 2025 holiday season! We also provide a few recommendations from holidays past and a few anti-recommendations.

title: Book Review: Saving 6 & Redeeming 6
description: This week we are talking about Joey and Aoife's love story in the Boys of Tommen series. While we love Johnny and Shannon these two characters (especially Joey) have a special place in our hearts. As always this episode contains major spoilers.

title: The Life of a Showgirl as Books
description: This week we are pairing each song off Taylor Swift's The Life of a Showgirl with books we think match the vibe. A few books discussed include the Boys of Tommen Series and Hexed.

title: If Books Had Honest Titles
description: This week we came up with honest titles to describe some of our most memorable reads. Books include Morning Glory Milking Farm, A Court of Mist and Fury, and The Wedding People. This episode contains major spoilers for several popular books.

title: October Book Club: Fall I Want
description: This week we are discussing our October book club pick, Fall I Want by Lyra Parish. This wasn't our favorite book club pick (for reasons we get into) but we did find it entertaining and if you are a fan of: fall, billionaire romance, or insta-love this might be for you! As always this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Book Review: The Love Hypothesis
description: This week we are talking about The Love Hypothesis- shocking I know since one of the lovely hosts has always said she wouldn't read it! Well she did and spoiler alert she loved it! As always this episode contains spoilers for the book so listen with caution.

title: Book Review: Gild, Glint and Gleam
description: This week we are discussing Gild, Glint and Gleam by Raven Kennedy. We are really enjoying this fantasy/romantasy series inspired by the King Midas myth and can't wait to see how it ends. Also we discuss the ribbons- I mean we have to. As always this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Spooky Season TBR & Recs
description: In this week's episode we discuss what is on our Halloween TBRs as well as discuss books we've enjoyed in Spooky Seasons' past. Books mentioned include Cackle, Witches Get Stitches, and Not the Witch You Wed.

title: September Book Club: Wrangled Love
description: In this week's episode we are discussing our September book club book, Wrangled Love by Ann Einerson! Overall we enjoyed this sweet billionaire/cowboy/single dad-nanny romance although we had a bone to pick with one of the plot points.

title: Author Interview: Adrian Andover
description: We have an exciting episode this week- our very first author interview! Adrian Andover just released his debut novel, Whiskey Business. Whiskey Business is a delightful cozy mystery and we had the pleasure of talking to Adrian about how this book came to be, his writing process, and his general love of books. You can pick up a copy from a variety of places but Adrian suggests getting it from your favorite indie bookstore, Hoopla, or your local library!

title: Bookish Fun
description: In this week's episode we decided to just have a bit of fun! We go through a few this or that questions about books and our reading habits and then we bring back a popular segment- blind ranking. Spoiler alert CJ was super mean to me again.

title: Fall TBR
description: Get comfy with a pumpkin spice latte and a cozy blanket- we're talking about our Fall TBRs this week! First we cover what (if anything) we actually read off our Summer TBR lists and then we get into the paranormal romance, thrillers, and exciting books we're hoping to read in the next few months.

title: Book Review: Tourist Trap
description: In today's episode we are discussing Tourist Trap by Morgan Elizabeth. We loved this spicy beach read and think you will too! As always this episode contains spoilers.

title: August Book Club: Things We Hide from the Light
description: This week we are talking about our August book club book, Things We Hide from the Light by Lucy Score. We have previously reviewed the first book in this series so check out that episode first! Overall we have continued to enjoy this series and find it a funny romantic suspense. As usual this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Book Review: Wild Love
description: This week we are discussing Wild Love by one of our favorite authors, Elsie Silver! We enjoyed this book as much as the other books we've read and loved by Elsie and are obsessing over how this series is totally focused on single dads. As always this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Book to Screen: Ballad of Songbirds and Snakes
description: This week we are discussing a book to screen adaptation- The Ballad of Songbirds and Snakes. We are both huge Hunger Games fans and have read all the books/watched all the movies. In this case we both agree- the book is better than the movie. As usual this episode contains major spoilers (for both the book and the movie).

title: July Book Club: The Wingman
description: This week we are discussing our July book club pick, The Wingman by Stephanie Archer! It is no secret we are huge fans of this series and hockey romance in general. As usual this episode contains spoilers.

title: Pairing Drinks with Books
description: This week we are pairing alcoholic drinks with different book tropes and giving examples of books that fit the vibe. Drinks include Long Island Iced Teas, Whiskey Sour, and Dirty Martinis. Enjoy this silly episode!

title: Book Review: Till Summer Do Us Part
description: In this week's episode we discuss Till Summer Do Us Part by Megan Quinn. This is a hilarious, spicy romcom about a fake marriage in trouble taking place at a marriage boot camp. It was ridiculous but also sweet. As always this episode contains spoilers.

title: Mid Year Check-in
description: In this week's episode we are doing a mid-year reading check-in. How many books have we read so far? What are our top genres? and most importantly- how close are we to completing our 25 books to read in 2025 lists?

title: June Book Club: Throne of Glass
description: This week we are discussing our June book club book- Throne of Glass by Sarah J Maas. This is CJ's first time reading the series and Holly's second so that makes for some interesting discussions as Holly tries not to spoil anything accidentally. However this discussion contains spoilers for Book 1 of the series and CJ shares some predictions for the rest of the series.

title: Summer Recommendations
description: We are following up our Summer 2025 TBR episode by sharing books we think should be on your summer TBR! We have a little of everything- romance, thrillers, fiction etc. A few books include Tourist Trap, Till Summer Do Us Part, and The Family Experiment

title: Bookish Propaganda We Aren't Falling For
description: We've been loving the propaganda we aren't falling for trend and had to make a bookish episode! Topics covered include AI, book buying bans, and of course third act breakups.

title: Book Review: Camera Shy
description: In this week's episode we discuss Camera Shy by Kay Cove. Buckle up for a bumpy ride because we have quite a few thoughts about this one (and the toxic side characters)

title: Summer TBR
description: This week we are reviewing what books are on our summer 2025 TBRs. Our lists include romance, mysteries, nonfiction, and women's fiction by favorite authors and new to us authors!

title: Book Review: Fight
description: This week we are reviewing Fight by Sloane St. James. We had such a fun time with this book about an EMT and a wild fire firefighter and will definitely be continuing the series. As always this episode contains spoilers.

title: Book Review: Just for the Summer
description: This week we are discussing Just for the Summer by Abby Jimenez! This is the perfect read to kick off your summer reading- it has humor, a sweet love story, and also a serious plot to go along with it! Both CJ and I really loved this one and instantly fell in love with the characters, the setting, and of course the author. As always this episode contains spoilers.

title: How Many Drinks...
description: In this week's episode we are discussing how many drinks it would take for us to hook up with book characters. We included some of our favorite book boyfriends (cough Christian Harper cough) as well as some diabolical options (the Bone Carver)

title: Part 2 Over and Under Hyped Books
description: We are back from our accidental time off with a part 2 of our Over vs. underhyped books (along with the bonus category of appropriately hyped books). We cover a wide range of genres- monster romance, cowboy romance, thrillers, fantasy, and historical fiction!

title: April Book Club: Deep End
description: This week we discuss our April book club pick- Deep End by Ali Hazelwood. It seems like everyone who reads this college sports romance either loves it or hates it- spoiler alert we love it! As always this episode contains major spoilers for the book so listen with caution.

title: Book Review: Bourbon & Lies
description: In this week's episode we review a small town, romantic suspense novel, Bourbon & Lies by Victoria Wilder. This was a quick, exciting and spicy read that we'd highly recommend. As always this episode contains spoilers.

title: New Favorite Authors
description: This week we are discussing some authors we have been loving lately, what we've read by them and what is on our TBR!

title: Book Review: Never Been Worse
description: This week we discuss "Never Been Worse", the latest release by one of our auto buy authors- Morgan Elizabeth. Spoiler alert: we loved every page of this book! As always this episode contains spoilers so listen with caution. And Morgan if you're listening we'd love to have you on the pod

title: March Book Club: Boarded Hearts
description: This week we discuss our March book club pick, Boarded Hearts by Ruth Stilling. While we gave the book similar ratings we actually had quite conflicting opinions! As always this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Reading One Star Reviews Part 2
description: This week we are back again reading One Star reviews of some of our favorite books. These goodreads reviews had us cracking up! Some of the books mentioned include Butcher & Blackbird, Reckless, Binding 13 and Never Been Worse. This episode contains spoilers.

title: We Listen and We Don't Judge
description: This week we have a special guest- our bestie Kylie! As our listeners know CJ and I love to ruin Kylie's day by telling her about all the ridiculous and messed up books we read. Sorry Kylie!

title: Spring TBR
description: In this week's episode we discuss what books are on our spring TBR. We cover a range of genres (although mostly romance of course) including fiction, mystery, fantasy, and monster romance.

title: Book Review: Ruthless Creatures & Carnal Urges
description: This week's episode is a double feature: Ruthless Creatures and Carnal Urges, the first two books in the Queens & Monsters series by J.T. Geissinger. This series is a mafia romance series and books 1 and 2 feature MMCs from the Russian and Irish mafias specifically so there are some darker themes and content. As always this episode contains spoilers so listen with caution

title: February Book Club: Onyx Storm
description: In this week's episode we discuss our February book club pick: Onyx Storm by Rebecca Yarros. As always this episode contains major spoilers for the whole series so listen with caution if you haven't read Fourth Wing, Iron Flame and Onyx Storm yet. We also celebrate our 1 year anniversary of Book Club After Dark!

title: Book Review: All My Love
description: In this week's episode we discuss All My Love by Morgan Elizabeth! All My Love is the first book in the Atlas Oaks series and is a second chance, childhood friends to lovers rockstar romance. Please check trigger warnings prior to reading because heavy topics are discussed. As per usual this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Lover Album as Books
description: This week in honor of Valentine's Day we are matching all of the songs on Taylor Swift's album Lover with books we've read (and loved). Featured books include Lovelight Farms, Play Along, and The Hunger Games.

title: Book Review: Zodiac Academy Part 2
description: In this week's episode we are continuing our discussion of the Zodiac Academy series. If you missed our last discussion- check out our November book club episode! Topics in this one include Xavier, Darius' deal with the stars, and Orion's trip to jail. As always this episode contains spoilers. For bonus content we also included our draft for next week's episode- the Lover album as books.

title: January Book Club: The Games Gods Play
description: This week's episode is all about our January book club pick- The Games Gods Play by Abigail Owen. We thoroughly enjoyed this fantasy romance and think you will too- especially if you enjoy books like Percy Jackson and The Hunger Games. As always this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Stuff Your Kindle Day
description: In preparation for the next Stuff Your Kindle day later this month we are telling you all about our experience with Stuff Your Kindle Days past- including a 5 star read! We also give a little haul of what we we picked up in the December event- including a whole lot of dark romance and monster romance

title: Fourth Wing, Iron Flame & Onyx Storm Theories
description: In this week's episode we are discussing all the theories we've heard regarding Fourth Wing, Iron Flame, and Onyx Storm as we anticipate the release of Onyx Storm later this month. We also discuss a few of our general thoughts and reactions on the series so far. As always this episode contains spoilers so listen with caution.

title: 2024 reading wrap-up
description: Happy New Year! In this week' episode we are discussing our 2024 wrap-up. We discuss our 5 star reads, how much we saved from using Kindle Unlimited and the library, and handed out some book superlatives. We are so excited to see what 2025 has in store for us

title: December book club: How the Grump Saved Christmas
description: This week we are discussing our December book club pick- How the Grump Saved Christmas by Claire Kingsley. This book is the perfect holiday season read for anyone who loves a Hallmark movie (and also squirrels) As always this episode contains major spoilers for the book.

title: 25 in 2025
description: In this week's episode we discuss the 25 books we each want to read in 2025. We also go over how we did in 2024 (not well), general goals for 2025, and predictions we have for our reading year

title: Book Review: Lights Out
description: In this week's episode we review Lights Out by Navessa Allen. Lights Out is a dark romantic comedy following a trauma nurse and her stalker who also happens to be her favorite masked social media star. This book had us laughing out loud and swooning over Josh. As always this episode contains spoilers

title: Our Holiday TBR
description: For this week's episode we are talking about our TBR for holiday romances this December as well as a few recs from Christmases past. This episode is perfectly paired with a cup of cocoa and a cozy blanket on your couch facing your tree.

title: November Book Club: Zodiac Academy
description: In this week's episode we discuss our November book club pick which was book 1 of Zodiac Academy- The Awakening. However, we are obsessed and couldn't put the series down so this episode also includes bonus discussion of books 2-5 in the series. Don't worry there will definitely be more episodes on this series in the future. As always this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Book Review: Archer
description: In this week's episode we discuss book 1 of the Reynold's Protective Agency Series: Archer by Dr. Rebecca Sharp! This book has it all- a protective MMC, a badass FMC who is on the run from the Boston Mob, meddling family, and a cozy small town setting. As always this episode contains spoilers for the book so listen at your own risk

title: Reading One Star Reviews
description: In this week's episode we are reading one star reviews of some of our favorite books. Books include ACOTAR, Fourth Wing, Twisted Love, and Reckless (among others). We were laughing uncontrollably at some of these hilarious reviews (and some made some valid points)

title: Overhyped vs Underhyped books
description: In this week's episode we are talking about books we think are overhyped and ones we think are critically underhyped (and of course books we think are accurately hyped). Please note just because we say a book is overhyped doesn't mean we don't like it usually they are books we quite enjoy!

title: October Book Club: Leather & Lark
description: This week's episode is focused on our October book club pick: Leather & Lark by Brynne Weaver. We adored Butcher & Blackbird and Leather & Lark was one of our most anticipated reads of 2024 so this was a great book club pick. As always this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Book Review: Alive and Wells
description: In this week's episode we discuss Alive and Wells by Bailey Hannah. This is another Canadian Cowboy romance and we enjoyed it very much! This one does have some heavier themes so check trigger warnings before reading and as always this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Halloween Recommendations
description: In this week's episode we discuss some of our favorite books to read for the spooky season! We included supernatural romances, dark romances, witchy reads, books that remind us of Halloween, and thrillers. We tried to include a book for all the readers this spooky season. Happy Haunting!

title: Best of Small Town Romance
description: In this week's episode CJ and Holly discuss all things small town romance (with a heavy dose of cowboy romance). Some shout outs include Heartless, Done and Dusted, All My Love, and It Happened One Summer.

title: Best of: College Romance
description: In this week's episode Holly and CJ discuss some of their favorite college romance books. The vast majority happen to also be sports romance so if that is what you like- we deliver plenty of solid recommendations. Highlights include the Off-Campus Series, Secret Puck, Only When It's Us and The Frat Boy

title: September Book Club: Passenger Princess
description: In this week's episode we discuss the September book club pick- Passenger Princess by Morgan Elizabeth. Morgan Elizabeth is one of our auto-buy authors so spoiler alert we thoroughly enjoyed this Miss Congeniality inspired rom-com. As always this episode contains spoilers so listen with caution.

title: Book Review: Binding and Keeping 13
description: In this week's episode CJ & Holly discuss their latest obsession- Binding 13 & Keeping 13 by Chloe Walsh. These books follow Johnny, a promising rugby star, and Shannon, a shy new student at Tommen College with secrets as they meet, become friends, and fall in love. As always this episode contains major spoilers.

title: Our Fall TBR
description: In this week's episode we discuss ten books on each of our fall TBRs. Fall is the perfect season for reading fantasy and all things spooky but of course who would we be if we didn't include a little romance?

title: Weird Books We've Read
description: In this week's episode we have a special guest- our best friend Kylie! One of our favorite things to do is to horrify her with the strange books we've been reading lately so we thought we'd share the fun with all of you! Books we have Kylie react to include Ice Planet Barbarians, Morning Glory Milking Farm, Stiff and of course Priest among others. Please note this episode contains spoilers so please listen with caution

title: Book Review: The Off-Campus Series
description: In this week's episode CJ and Holly discuss the Off Campus Series by Elle Kennedy. This episode contains spoilers as always. We especially love The Deal and The Score (love Wellsy and Garrett and Dean and Allie!)

title: August Book Club: Play Along
description: In this week's episode CJ and Holly discuss the August book club pick- Play Along by Liz Tomforde. This is book 4 in the Windy City series and was one of our most anticipated reads of the year. Spoiler alert: It did not disappoint. As always this episode contains spoilers.

title: Tortured Poets Department as Books
description: In this week's episode CJ and Holly match each of the songs from the first half of Taylor Swift's Tortured Poets Department Album with books that match their vibe or certain lyrics. Some highlight songs for this episode include Down Bad, Clara Bow and The Alchemy.

title: Mid Year Reading Check-in
description: In this week's episode Holly and CJ do a mid-year reading check-in. How many books from their 24 in 2024 lists have they read? How close are they to their reading goal? What 5 star reads have they had so far?

title: July Book Club: Daughter of No Worlds
description: In this week's episode CJ and Holly discuss their July book club pick, Daughter of No Worlds by Carissa Broadbent. This book was voted on by you lovely listeners over on our Instagram @bookclub.afterdark. It is the first in a fantasy trilogy following a former slave and a reclusive magic wielder as they try their hardest to bring justice to the world. As always this episode contains spoilers.

title: Book Review: Mile High
description: In this week's episode CJ and Holly discuss book 1 of the Windy City Series, Mile High, by Liz Tomforde (finally). Topics include some of our favorite scenes of the book, why we love Stevie and Zanders so much and of course our overall love for Liz as an author. As always this episode contains spoilers.

title: Book Review: Haunting and Hunting Adeline
description: Buckle up: in this week's episode Holly and CJ discuss the Cat and Mouse duology. Fun fact this is actually the first book Holly and CJ read together as a book club (because they were so scared of the books). Please check trigger warnings for the series before listening to this episode as many serious and distressing topics are discussed. This is a DARK romance. As always this episode contains spoilers

title: Blind rankings
description: In this week’s episode we blindly rank books, book boyfriends, authors, tropes and sub genres. We also included a few nightmare rounds of all bad options for some extra laughs

title: Our auto-buy authors
description: In this week’s episode we discuss some of our auto-buy authors. Every time they release something new we are lining up to buy it or read it. Some authors mentioned include Liz Tomforde, Ana Huang and Elsie Silver.

title: Book Review: It Happened One Summer & Hook Line and Sinker
description: In this week’s episode Holly and CJ discuss two popular books by Tessa Bailey: It Happened One Summer and Hook, Line and Sinker. Both are super fun summer reads although It Happened One Summer was our favorite of the two. As always this episode contains spoilers so listen at your own risk.

title: June Book Club: Things We Never Got Over
description: In this week’s episode Holly and CJ discuss the June book club pick Things We Never Got Over by Lucy Score. As always this episode contains spoilers for the book so listen with caution.

title: Our Summer Reading Lists
description: In this week’s episode CJ and Holly discuss 10 books on both of their summer TBRs as well as a few recommendations of books you should pick up this summer

title: Book review: Bergman Brothers books 1-3
description: In this week’s episode CJ and Holly discuss the first 3 Bergman Brothers books: only when it’s us, always only you, and ever after always by Chloe Liese. Chloe is a phenomenal author and this is such a heart warming, fun romance series. As always this episode contains minor spoilers

title: He’s a 10 but…
description: In this week’s episode CJ and Holly rank things book boyfriends do out of context. For example: he’s a 10 but he can darken his eyes or he’s a 10 but he can play the fiddle (cough tamlin cough). We had a ton of fun recording this week’s episode (maybe too much) and hope you enjoy listening

title: May Book Club Pick: Priest
description: In this week’s episode CJ and Holly discuss the May pick for Book Club After Dark: Priest. Buckle up this one’s a doozy with a heavy dose of sacrilege. As always this episode contains spoilers.

title: All About Hockey Romance
description: In this week’s episode CJ and Holly talk all things hockey romance: what they love, what they don’t and several recommendations on getting started in the genre. This episode also includes a lot of fangirling over Liz Tomforde so be warned (and Liz if you’re listening we love you)

title: 1989 (Taylor’s Version) as books
description: In this week’s episode CJ and Holly pair songs from 1989 with books. Some highlights from this episode include Slut! And Welcome to New York

title: April book club: Hot Vampire Next Door
description: In this week’s episode Holly and CJ discuss the April book club pick for Book Club After Dark: books 1 and 2 of Hot Vampire Next Door. This episode contains spoilers for books 1 and 2 of this novella series. Each month’s book club pick is selected from a poll on the bookclub.afterdark Instagram page.

title: Book Review: Verity
description: In this week’s episode CJ and Holly discuss Verity by Colleen Hoover. Verity is a romantic suspense novel made popular by booktok. Please note this episode contains major spoilers for the book so listen at your own risk. Additionally, please look up trigger warnings as this is a very dark book

title: Our Kindle Unlimited Starter Guide
description: In this week’s episode Holly and CJ discuss all things Kindle unlimited! Starting with how much they’ve each saved using KU and going through some of their top KU reads including Tis the Season for Revenge, Dreamland Billionaires, Funny Feelings, and Ice Planet Barbarians to name a few

title: Fanfiction
description: In this week’s episode Holly and CJ discuss a genre of literature near and dear to many readers hearts- fan fiction. Join your hosts as they discuss what got them into fan fiction in the first place (cough One Direction cough), some of the best fan fics they’ve read, and why they love fan fiction so much

title: Book Review: ACOTAR & ACOMAF
description: In this week’s episode CJ and Holly discuss their thoughts, feelings and opinions on A Court of Thorns and Roses and A Court of Mist and Fury. What to expect from this episode- lots of bashing on Tamlin, a healthy amount of calling characters Daddy, and just a little plot review. Please be aware this episode does contain spoilers for the first two books of the A Court of Thorns and Roses series.

title: In our Reputation Era
description: In this week’s episode CJ and Holly discuss books they think relate to all the songs on Taylor Swift’s Reputation album. Are you ready for it?

title: Romance book yays, IRL nays
description: In this week’s episode CJ and Holly discuss various tropes that they absolutely love in romance books but that would have them running the other way if they happened in real life. Buckle up this one is a wild (and funny) ride

title: Book Review: Icebreaker
description: In this week’s episode CJ and Holly discuss Icebreaker by Hannah Grace. This episode contains spoilers for the book if you haven’t already read it. Join as they discuss their love for Nate, their hate for Aaron and their memories of Uber rides in college

title: Book Review: Butcher & Blackbird
description: In today’s episode CJ & Holly discuss Butcher & Blackbird by Brynne Weaver. This episode does contain spoilers for several scenes throughout the book so if you haven’t read the book yet please be aware. This is a dark romantic comedy so please look at the trigger warnings for the book the author provides.

title: 24 books to read in 2024
description: In tonight’s episode CJ and Holly discuss their list of 24 books they hope to read in 2024! Highlights include new releases from favorite authors and popular booktok books!

title: Our 5 Star Reads of 2023
description: Welcome to Book Club After Dark! In today’s episode Holly and CJ discuss their top reads of last year (and some honorable mentions!)
